page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 6 shared vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, configured with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.67 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a performance profile that is highly variable depending on the workload type. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng score of 787.9 placing it in the bottom 10% of tested servers, and a weak multi-core stress-ng score of 4703.94 in the bottom 25%. However, memory subsystem performance is more resilient; the instance achieves average read-only memory bandwidth of 616.67 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 43.56 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks. Conversely, large-block memory write bandwidth is weak at 24.88 GB/sec. General compute benchmarks like Geekbench yield average scores, with 1351.0 for single-core and 5544.0 for multi-core operations.

This server is configured for workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources and do not require dedicated local storage. It demonstrates average performance in database operations, with a Passmark database score of 3941.27 and Redis SET operations reaching 8.81 million operations per second. It also handles basic text processing and software compilation with average efficiency. However, it is less suited for high-throughput web serving, where its static web serving throughput of 5.86 GB/sec falls into the bottom 25%. It is appropriate for development environments, lightweight database hosting, and general-purpose utility workloads where cost efficiency is prioritized over guaranteed, sustained CPU performance.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It features 6 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between compute and memory: while stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU scores fall into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively due to the shared CPU allocation, memory read bandwidth and Geekbench scores remain in the average tier. This resource profile is suitable for workloads such as lightweight database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose development tasks that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance or high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering average memory bandwidth alongside budget-tier shared CPU performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, designed for budget general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server balancing sixteen gigabytes of memory with budget-friendly, multi-core AMD EPYC processing for non-critical workloads.
bullet_points: 6 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with average read bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
KVM hypervisor architecture on x86_64 platform
Suited for lightweight databases, software compilation, and development
